description: This lecture provides a rigorous proof of the 33% impossibility result in blockchain consensus, explaining the conditions under which consistency and liveness can be achieved in a partially synchronous model.
topic_summary: The lecture discusses the impossibility result related to Byzantine agreement protocols, emphasizing the critical threshold of 33% Byzantine nodes that can disrupt consensus. It covers the implications of this threshold in both asynchronous and synchronous phases, and the necessity of achieving agreement among honest nodes.
